diagrams.title: Cristian's Algorithm Message Flow
diagrams.description: Shows client-server exchange with timestamps at each step for calculating synchronized time
elements: Server sends response with time T3
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how Cristian's algorithm estimates correct time accounting for network delay

diagrams.title: NTP Stratum Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Pyramid structure showing hierarchical organization of NTP servers from primary reference clocks
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how time information propagates through NTP hierarchy with decreasing accuracy

diagrams.title: Berkeley Algorithm Architecture
diagrams.description: Star topology showing master node coordinating with all slave nodes for synchronization
elements: Poll messages from master to slaves (request time)
Response messages from slaves to master
Adjustment messages back to all slaves
Values showing how average is computed
diagrams.purpose: Understanding master-slave approach and how average time is calculated and distributed

diagrams.title: Clock Drift Over Time
diagrams.description: Two parallel timelines showing perfect clock vs drifting clock with widening gap
elements: Resynchronization points where clocks are corrected
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how clocks diverge over time and need periodic resynchronization

diagrams.title: Four-Message NTP Exchange
diagrams.description: Detailed sequence diagram showing NTP symmetric mode with all four timestamp exchanges
elements: Equations for calculating offset and delay
diagrams.purpose: Understanding NTP's precise timestamp exchange and how it calculates clock offset and network delay

visuals.title: Synchronizing Physical Clocks
visuals.description: Visual diagram for synchronizing physical clocks
